phase.name: 1: API -- Add tag search and tagIds filter to all transaction GET routes

task.name: Add tag name to search OR clause and tagIds filter in expense-transactions GET
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors in the expense-transactions route.

task.name: Add tag name to search OR clause and tagIds filter in income-transactions GET
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors in the income-transactions route.

task.name: Add tag name to search OR clause and tagIds filter in transfer-transactions GET
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors in the transfer-transactions route.

phase.name: 2: Hooks -- Add tagIds to all transaction query hooks

task.name: Add tagIds support to useExpenseTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts`:
1. Add `tagIds?: string[]` to the `UseExpenseTransactionsOptions` interface.
2. Add `tagIds?: string[]` as a parameter to `fetchExpenseTransactions`.
3. In `fetchExpenseTransactions`, add: `if (tagIds && tagIds.length > 0) params.append('tagIds', tagIds.join(','));`
4. In `useExpenseTransactionsQuery`, destructure `tagIds` from options.
5. Pass `tagIds` to `fetchExpenseTransactions` in the queryFn.
6. Add `tagIds` to the query key object: `{ skip, take, search, dateFilter, accountId, tagIds }`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tagIds support to useIncomeTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts`:
Apply the same pattern as the expense hook: 1. Add `tagIds?: string[]` to `UseIncomeTransactionsOptions`. 2. Add `tagIds` parameter to `fetchIncomeTransactions`. 3. Append `tagIds` to URL params in the fetch function. 4. Destructure and pass `tagIds` through in the hook. 5. Add `tagIds` to the query key object.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tagIds support to useTransferTransactionsQuery
task.action:
In `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts`:
Apply the same pattern as the expense hook: 1. Add `tagIds?: string[]` to `UseTransfersOptions`. 2. Add `tagIds` parameter to `fetchTransfers`. 3. Append `tagIds` to URL params in the fetch function. 4. Destructure and pass `tagIds` through in the hook. 5. Add `tagIds` to the query key object.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Ensure Checkbox UI component exists
task.action: Check if `src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx` exists. If not, generate it: ``` npx shadcn@latest add checkbox ```
task.verify: Confirm `src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx` exists and exports a `Checkbox` component.

task.name: Create TagFilter component
task.action:
Create `src/components/shared/TagFilter.tsx` with the following:
**Props interface:** ```typescript interface TagFilterProps { selectedTagIds: string[]; onChange: (tagIds: string[]) => void; disabled?: boolean; } ```
**Implementation:** 1. Import `useTagsQuery` to get all user tags internally. 2. Use Shadcn `Popover` + `PopoverTrigger` + `PopoverContent` for the dropdown. 3. Trigger button: `Button variant="outline"` with a `Tags` icon from lucide-react. Label shows "Tags" when no selection, "Tags (N)" when N tags selected. 4. Popover content: A scrollable list (max-h-[200px] overflow-y-auto) of tag items. Each item is a row with a `Checkbox`, a colored dot (`span` with `backgroundColor` set to the tag's HSL color), and the tag name. Clicking toggles the tag in the selection. 5. Include a search input at the top of the popover to filter the tag list within the popover (local filter only, does not affect transaction results). 6. Include a "Clear all" button at the bottom when any tags are selected. 7. Below the Popover (outside it), render active filter pills as `Badge variant="secondary"` components with the tag name and an X button to deselect. 8. If the user has no tags, render the button as disabled with `title="No tags created yet"`.
Follow existing component patterns: named export, use Shadcn primitives, TypeScript strict.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors. Visually verify the component renders correctly by temporarily adding it to a page.

phase.name: 4: Desktop tables -- Add tag text search and TagFilter UI

task.name: Add tag filtering to ExpenseTable
task.action:
In `src/components/tables/expenses/ExpenseTable.tsx`:
1. Import `TagFilter` from `@/components/shared/TagFilter`.
2. Add state: `const [selectedTagIds, setSelectedTagIds] = useState<string[]>([]);`
3. In the `filteredData` useMemo, add two filtering additions:
a. **Tag text search** -- In the search filter return statement, add: `row.tags?.some(tag => tag.name.toLowerCase().includes(searchLower))` to the existing OR chain.
b. **Tag ID filter** -- Before the search filter check, add: ```typescript if (selectedTagIds.length > 0) { const hasMatchingTag = row.tags?.some(tag => selectedTagIds.includes(tag.id)); if (!hasMatchingTag) return false; } ```
4. Add `selectedTagIds` to the `filteredData` useMemo dependency array.
5. In the JSX toolbar (the `div` with `flex items-center justify-between`), add the `TagFilter` component between the date ToggleGroup and the search InputGroup: ```tsx <TagFilter selectedTagIds={selectedTagIds} onChange={setSelectedTagIds} /> ```
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors. Verify the tag filter appears in the ExpenseTable toolbar.

task.name: Add tag filtering to IncomeTable
task.action:
In `src/components/tables/income/IncomeTable.tsx`:
Apply the same changes as ExpenseTable: 1. Import `TagFilter`. 2. Add `selectedTagIds` state. 3. Add tag text search and tag ID filter to `filteredData` useMemo. 4. Add `selectedTagIds` to the useMemo dependency array. 5. Add `TagFilter` component to the toolbar.
Note: Check the specific field names -- IncomeTable uses `incomeType` instead of `expenseType`, so verify the search filter additions use the correct field names.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tag filtering to TransferTable
task.action:
In `src/components/tables/transfers/TransferTable.tsx`:
Apply the same changes as ExpenseTable: 1. Import `TagFilter`. 2. Add `selectedTagIds` state. 3. Add tag text search and tag ID filter to `filteredData` useMemo. 4. Add `selectedTagIds` to the useMemo dependency array. 5. Add `TagFilter` component to the toolbar.
Note: TransferTable uses `transferType`, `fromAccount`, `toAccount`, and `notes` instead of `description`. Verify the search filter additions use the correct field names.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

phase.name: 5: Mobile views -- Add TagFilter UI and pass tagIds to hooks

task.name: Add tag filter to expenses page (mobile)
task.action:
In `src/app/expenses/page.tsx`:
1. Import `TagFilter` from `@/components/shared/TagFilter`. 2. Add state: `const [selectedTagIds, setSelectedTagIds] = useState<string[]>([]);` 3. Pass `tagIds: selectedTagIds` to the `useExpenseTransactionsQuery` hook options (alongside existing `search`, `dateFilter`, etc.). 4. In the mobile view section (`md:hidden`), render `TagFilter` between the search `InputGroup` and the date `ToggleGroup`: ```tsx <TagFilter selectedTagIds={selectedTagIds} onChange={setSelectedTagIds} disabled={isLoading} /> ``` 5. Update the `isFiltering` check to include tag filter: `selectedTagIds.length > 0`. 6. Add a `useEffect` to reset `displayCount` when `selectedTagIds` changes (same pattern as existing search/dateFilter resets).
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tag filter to income page (mobile)
task.action:
In `src/app/income/page.tsx`:
Apply the same pattern as the expenses page: 1. Import `TagFilter`. 2. Add `selectedTagIds` state. 3. Pass `tagIds: selectedTagIds` to `useIncomeTransactionsQuery`. 4. Render `TagFilter` in the mobile section between search and date toggle. 5. Update `isFiltering` to include tag filter. 6. Add `useEffect` to reset `displayCount` when `selectedTagIds` changes.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tag filter to transfers page (mobile)
task.action:
In `src/app/transfers/page.tsx`:
Apply the same pattern as the expenses page: 1. Import `TagFilter`. 2. Add `selectedTagIds` state. 3. Pass `tagIds: selectedTagIds` to `useTransfersQuery`. 4. Render `TagFilter` in the mobile section between search and date toggle. 5. Update `isFiltering` to include tag filter. 6. Add `useEffect` to reset `displayCount` when `selectedTagIds` changes.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tag filter to TransactionsMobileView
task.action:
In `src/components/transactions/TransactionsMobileView.tsx`:
1. Import `TagFilter` from `@/components/shared/TagFilter`. 2. Add three `selectedTagIds` states (one per tab): ```typescript const [expenseSelectedTagIds, setExpenseSelectedTagIds] = useState<string[]>([]); const [incomeSelectedTagIds, setIncomeSelectedTagIds] = useState<string[]>([]); const [transferSelectedTagIds, setTransferSelectedTagIds] = useState<string[]>([]); ``` 3. Pass `tagIds` to each hook call: - `useExpenseTransactionsQuery({ ..., tagIds: expenseSelectedTagIds })` - `useIncomeTransactionsQuery({ ..., tagIds: incomeSelectedTagIds })` - `useTransfersQuery({ ..., tagIds: transferSelectedTagIds })` 4. In each tab's JSX section, render `TagFilter` between the search InputGroup and date ToggleGroup. 5. Update the `isFiltering` variables to include tag filter state: - `isExpenseFiltering` should include `expenseSelectedTagIds.length > 0` - `isIncomeFiltering` should include `incomeSelectedTagIds.length > 0` - `isTransferFiltering` should include `transferSelectedTagIds.length > 0` 6. Add `useEffect` hooks to reset each display count when its corresponding `selectedTagIds` changes.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` and confirm no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Full build and lint check
task.action: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build` to verify zero errors across the entire project.
task.verify: Both commands exit with code 0 and no errors.

task.name: Generate verification document
task.action: Create `docs/search-by-tags-verification.md` documenting: 1. All files created or modified. 2. Build and lint results. 3. Manual verification steps performed (or instructions for the user to verify): - Text search matching tag names works on all 3 transaction types. - Tag filter selects/deselects correctly. - OR logic works (selecting multiple tags shows transactions with any of them). - Tag filter + date filter + text search combine correctly (AND). - Mobile and desktop layouts both show the tag filter. - Removing all selected tags shows all transactions again.
task.verify: File exists at `docs/search-by-tags-verification.md` with complete content.
